query: France Ebola case 2026: current status of the doctor who tested positive after aid mission, any secondary cases, ECDC reporting. How many confirmed Ebola cases in France as of July 2026?
answer:
Research complete. Here is the summary:

**As of July 2026, France has exactly ONE (1) confirmed Ebola case** — with zero secondary cases.

**Key findings:**

- **The case**: A humanitarian doctor affiliated with the NGO **ALIMA** tested positive for **Bundibugyo ebolavirus** on **24 June 2026** after flying commercial (Air France) from the DRC into Charles de Gaulle Airport on 23 June. This was the first-ever Ebola case on French soil and the first to test positive outside Africa in the 2026 outbreak.

- **Current status of the doctor**: **Fully recovered and discharged on 4 July 2026** after two negative PCR tests, per Health Minister Stéphanie Rist. He was nearly asymptomatic (only headaches).

- **Secondary cases**: **None** — zero in France or anywhere in the EU/EEA. Five flight passengers were identified as contacts and isolated as a precaution; contact tracing found no onward transmission. The case is cited as a successfully contained European Ebola case.

- **ECDC reporting**: Tracked via ECDC's weekly Communicable Disease Threats Reports (Weeks 26–27, 2026) and its dedicated outbreak page (updated 10 July 2026). ECDC assessed EU/EEA infection likelihood as **"very low."**

- **Corroboration**: WHO's Disease Outbreak News (2026-DON612) explicitly counts "one from France (linked to DRC)."

For context, the underlying DRC outbreak (declared a PHEIC on 16 May 2026) had 1,792 confirmed cases/625 deaths in DRC and 20 cases/2 deaths in Uganda as of 10 July 2026.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 10 July 2026: one imported case in France (humanitarian doctor), no EU/EEA secondary cases; EU/EEA infection likelihood very low. DRC 1,792 cases/625 deaths; Uganda 20 cases/2 deaths.

key_facts: France confirmed its first-ever Ebola case on national territory on 24 June 2026; it was the first case to test positive on European soil during the 2026 outbreak.
The patient was a humanitarian doctor affiliated with the NGO ALIMA who returned from a mission in the DRC, flying commercial (Air France) into Charles de Gaulle Airport on 23 June 2026.
The virus involved is the rarer Bundibugyo ebolavirus strain.
The doctor fully recovered and was discharged from hospital on 4 July 2026 after two negative PCR tests; he was nearly asymptomatic (only headaches).
There have been ZERO secondary cases in France or the EU/EEA.
Five Air France flight passengers were identified as potential contacts and placed in precautionary isolation; no onward transmission occurred.
As of July 2026, France has exactly ONE (1) confirmed Ebola case.
WHO Disease Outbreak News (2026-DON612) counted 'one from France (linked to DRC)' among total outbreak cases.
ECDC reported the case via weekly Communicable Disease Threats Reports and its outbreak page, assessing EU/EEA infection likelihood as 'very low.'
Broader outbreak (ECDC, 10 July 2026): 1,792 confirmed cases and 625 deaths in DRC; 20 cases and 2 deaths in Uganda.

data_points.metric: Confirmed Ebola cases in France (as of July 2026)
Secondary Ebola cases in France / EU-EEA
Air France flight passengers identified as contacts and isolated
Negative PCR tests before patient discharge
